title: An Introduction to Industrial Property (Patents and Utility models)
author: Barbieri, Massimo
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Intellectual property (IP) is the management of something volatile and intrinsically risky: a patent application can be rejected, and if granted, its validity can be challenged by a third party. 

 There are different forms of industrial property: patents, utility models, industrial designs, and trademarks. Each has its scope of protection and limitations. 

 A focus on patents and the patenting procedure at Politecnico di Milano is carried out. 

 The criteria applied in the evaluation process include the technical feasibility of an invention, the patentability requirements, and the assessment of a reasonable probability that an invention can be transferred to industry. 

 The evaluation of novelty and inventive steps requires a detailed description of an invention and a list of advantages compared to the state of the art. 

 The main patent strategies are also discussed. The first step is the submission of a priority patent application in Italy. After the national filing, there is one year to decide whether to extend or not the patent. Usually, a PCT application is filed, because it is an option for future patenting that provides an applicant with a further delay before deciding to apply or not the more expensive national filings.
steps_raw:
Intellectual property (IP) is the management of something volatile and intrinsically risky: a patent application can be rejected, and if granted, its validity can be challenged by a third party. 

 There are different forms of industrial property: patents, utility models, industrial designs, and trademarks. Each has its scope of protection and limitations. 

 A focus on patents and the patenting procedure at Politecnico di Milano is carried out. 

 The criteria applied in the evaluation process include the technical feasibility of an invention, the patentability requirements, and the assessment of a reasonable probability that an invention can be transferred to industry. 

 The evaluation of novelty and inventive steps requires a detailed description of an invention and a list of advantages compared to the state of the art. 

 The main patent strategies are also discussed. The first step is the submission of a priority patent application in Italy. After the national filing, there is one year to decide whether to extend or not the patent. Usually, a PCT application is filed, because it is an option for future patenting that provides an applicant with a further delay before deciding to apply or not the more expensive national filings.
